rule: All food workers, including wait staff, should know how to prepare and handle food safely to prevent food-related illnesses.
nlc: Verify that all food workers, including wait staff, know how to prepare and handle food safely to prevent food-related illnesses.

rule: Owners and managers should study this guide, share it with their employees and use the Self-Inspection Worksheet (after page 12) to regularly check their restaurants’ physical and environmental conditions and their employees’ food-handling practices.
nlc: Verify that restaurant owners and managers have studied this guide, shared it with their employees, and are using the Self-Inspection Worksheet to regularly check their restaurants' physical and environmental conditions as well as their employees' food-handling practices.

rule: The Health Code requires food service establishments to have a supervisor of food operations with a Food Protection Certificate on duty during all hours of operation to supervise food preparation and processing.
nlc: Check if there is a supervisor of food operations with a Food Protection Certificate on duty during all hours of operation to supervise food preparation and processing at the food service establishment.

rule: The Health Department recommends that restaurants have more than one staff person with a Food Protection Certificate.
nlc: Verify that restaurants have more than one staff person with a Food Protection Certificate as recommended by The Health Department.

rule: Individuals can earn a Certificate by taking and passing the Health Department’s food protection course.
nlc: Verify that individuals can earn a Certificate by taking and passing the Health Department’s food protection course.

rule: Every food service establishment in New York City receives an unannounced, onsite inspection at least once a year to check if it is meeting Health Code food safety requirements.
nlc: Verify that every food service establishment in New York City receives an unannounced, onsite inspection at least once a year to check if it is meeting Health Code food safety requirements.

rule: The inspector may visit anytime the restaurant is receiving or preparing food or drink, or is open to the public.
nlc: Check if the inspector may visit anytime the restaurant is receiving or preparing food or drink, or is open to the public.

rule: Health Department inspectors hold bachelor’s degrees with significant coursework in science.
nlc: Check if Health Department inspectors hold bachelor's degrees with significant coursework in science.

rule: All inspectors undergo months of intense public health and communications instruction before they conduct an inspection on their own.
nlc: Verify that all inspectors undergo months of intense public health and communications instruction before they conduct an inspection on their own.

rule: They are taught to identify and explain to operators which hazards contribute to food-borne illnesses and to document these accurately in an inspection report.
nlc: Verify that they identify and explain to operators which hazards contribute to food-borne illnesses and document these accurately in an inspection report.

rule: It is a crime to offeror for the inspector to demand money, gifts or services of any kind.
nlc: Verify that no money, gifts, or services of any kind are offered or demanded by an inspector, as it is a crime.

rule: Legal action will be taken against anyone who offers or accepts a bribe.
nlc: Verify that legal action is taken against anyone who offers or accepts a bribe.

rule: The inspector records observed violations in a handheld computer during the inspection.
nlc: Verify that the inspector records observed violations in a handheld computer during the inspection.

rule: Each violation is associated with a range of points depending on the type and extent of the violation, and the risk it poses to the public.
nlc: Verify that each violation is associated with a range of points that vary depending on the type, extent, and risk to the public.

rule: At the end of the inspection, the points are added together for an inspection score.
nlc: Verify that, at the end of the inspection, the points are added together for an inspection score.

rule: Lower inspection scores indicate better compliance with the Health Code.
nlc: Verify that lower inspection scores indicate better compliance with the Health Code.

rule: Health Code violations are classified as “critical” or “general”.
nlc: Verify that health code violations are classified as "critical" or "general".

rule: Critical violations are more likely than general ones to contribute to food-borne illnesses because they may be a substantial risk to the public’s health.
nlc: Verify that critical violations, which pose a substantial risk to public health, are more likely than general ones to contribute to food-borne illnesses.

rule: Critical violations are given more points than general violations.
nlc: Verify that critical violations are assigned more points than general violations.

rule: Failing to cook food to required temperatures is a critical violation, while failing to provide an accurate thermometer in a refrigerator is a general violation.
nlc: Verify that failing to cook food to required temperatures is a critical violation, and failing to provide an accurate thermometer in a refrigerator is a general violation.

rule: “Public health hazards” are critical violations that pose an immediate health threat. Due to their serious nature, they carry the most points.
nlc: Verify that "public health hazards" are identified as critical violations that pose an immediate health threat, and ensure they carry the most points due to their serious nature.

rule: If an establishment does not correct a public health hazard before the end of the inspection, the Health Department may close it immediately.
nlc: Verify that an establishment that did not correct a public health hazard before the end of the inspection was immediately closed by the Health Department.

rule: "Pre-permit serious items” are critical violations in the design of an establishment, such as improper sewage disposal or lack of a hand-washing facility near the food preparation area. The Health Department will not issue a permit if the establishment has one of these conditions.
nlc: Verify that the establishment does not have any critical pre-permit serious items, such as improper sewage disposal or a lack of a hand-washing facility near the food preparation area, as the Health Department will not issue a permit under these conditions.

rule: These violations must be corrected before the restaurant opens, or immediately if the establishment is already open to the public. Because these violations are critical to safe design and operation of the food service establishment, they carry the most points.
nlc: Verify that critical violations are corrected before the restaurant opens, or immediately if the establishment is already open to the public, as these violations are critical to safe design and operation and carry the most points.

rule: The number of points an establishment receives for a critical or general violation depends on its condition level, meaning the extent and frequency of the violation.
nlc: Check if the number of points an establishment receives for a critical or general violation depends on its condition level, meaning the extent and frequency of the violation.

rule: Every condition level is determined by a specific set of parameters.
nlc: Verify that every condition level is determined by a specific set of parameters.

rule: Conditions range from Level I, which carries the fewest points, to Level V, which carries the most points.
nlc: Verify that the conditions carrying the fewest points are classified as Level I and that the conditions carrying the most points are classified as Level V.

rule: Condition Level V, in most instances, is used to score public health hazards that are not corrected at the time of the inspection and is usually assigned 28 points.
nlc: Check if Condition Level V is assigned 28 points for public health hazards that are not corrected at the time of inspection.

rule: Some cited violations may result in a Notice of Violation, fine and/or follow-up inspection, but are not counted toward the inspection score.
nlc: Verify that cited violations, which may result in a Notice of Violation, fine, and/or follow-up inspection, are not counted toward the inspection score.

rule: At the end of the inspection, the inspector will: - Review the results of the sanitary inspection with the operator, explain violations and suggest ways to correct them and improve food safety.
nlc: Verify that at the end of the inspection, the inspector reviews the results of the sanitary inspection with the operator, explains violations, and suggests ways to correct them and improve food safety.

rule: Cited violations should be corrected as soon as possible, and the establishment should take steps to prevent them from recurring.
nlc: Check if cited violations are corrected as soon as possible, and that the establishment has taken steps to prevent them from recurring.

rule: Issue a printed Inspection Report that states what the inspector observed, the violation points and the inspection score.
nlc: Verify that a printed Inspection Report is issued stating what the inspector observed, the violation points, and the inspection score.

rule: Issue a Notice of Violation if the score was 14 or more points in general violations.
nlc: Verify that a Notice of Violation was issued if the score was 14 or more points in general violations.

rule: The inspector may issue a Notice of Violation if a critical violation or an unscored violation was cited.
nlc: Check if a Notice of Violation was issued when a critical violation or an unscored violation was cited.

rule: All Notices of Violation are signed by the inspector and the food service operator.
nlc: Verify that each Notice of Violation has been signed by the inspector and the food service operator.

rule: Provide the date when the Notice of Violation will be heard by a hearing examiner at the Health Hearings Division of the Office of Trials and Hearings (OATH).
nlc: Verify that the date when the Notice of Violation will be heard by a hearing examiner at the Health Hearings Division of the Office of Trials and Hearings (OATH) is provided.

rule: The back of the Notice of Violation includes contact information for OATH, a description of the hearing process and information about settlement and hearing by mail.
nlc: Verify that the back of the Notice of Violation includes contact information for OATH, a description of the hearing process, and information about settlement and hearing by mail.

rule: Fines are determined at OATH. They range from 200 to 2,000 per violation and may be higher for repeated violations.
nlc: Verify that fines determined at OATH range from $200 to $2,000 per violation and may be higher for repeated violations.

rule: Use the Self-Inspection Worksheet and Guide to Conditions in this booklet to conduct regular self-inspections.
nlc: Verify that regular self-inspections are conducted using the Self-Inspection Worksheet and Guide to Conditions in this booklet.

rule: The Health Department may order a restaurant to temporarily close to correct a public health hazard that cannot be corrected before the end of an inspection or when the restaurant is operating without a valid permit.
nlc: Verify that a restaurant is ordered to temporarily close if a public health hazard could not be corrected before the end of an inspection or if the restaurant was operating without a valid permit.

rule: A restaurant may also be closed if it scores 28 or more points on three consecutive inspections.
nlc: Verify that a restaurant was closed if it scored 28 or more points on three consecutive inspections.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE r1.consecutive_high_scores >= 3 AND r1.action NOT LIKE '%Closed by DOHMH%'

rule: Prior to closure, an inspector will contact a supervisor, who will determine whether to order the establishment closed.
nlc: Verify that, prior to closure, an inspector contacted a supervisor, who determined whether to order the establishment closed.

rule: If it is closed, Health Department closure signs must be immediately posted in the window(s) and/or door(s), all operations must cease and the restaurant must remain closed for business until it is authorized by the Health Department to reopen.
nlc: Check if the establishment, if closed by the Health Department, has immediately posted closure signs in the window(s) and/or door(s), ceased all operations, and is remaining closed for business until the Health Department authorizes its reopening.

rule: The Department will monitor the establishment to ensure it remains closed and will issue additional violations for not complying with the closing order.
nlc: Verify that the establishment remains closed and that additional violations are issued if it does not comply with the closing order.

rule: To reopen, the establishment must submit a written statement to the Health Department indicating that it has corrected all the violations that led to its being closed.
nlc: Verify that, to reopen, the establishment has submitted a written statement to the Health Department indicating that it has corrected all the violations that led to its being closed.

rule: The operator may be asked to attend an informal meeting with Health Department supervisors.
nlc: Check if the operator may be asked to attend an informal meeting with Health Department supervisors.

rule: If it appears that sanitary conditions have improved, an inspector will conduct a reopening inspection while the establishment remains closed to the public.
nlc: Verify that, if it appears sanitary conditions have improved, an inspector conducts a reopening inspection while the establishment remains closed to the public.

rule: Health Department supervisors will determine whether it may reopen.
nlc: Check if Health Department supervisors determine whether it may reopen.

rule: After re-opening, the establishment will be inspected for compliance with the Health Code. If it is in sufficient compliance, it may remain open and will be inspected again in about three months.
nlc: Verify that after re-opening, the establishment was inspected for Health Code compliance, and if founded in sufficient compliance, it remained open and was inspected again in about three months.

rule: Repeated violations may prompt the Health Department to initiate procedures for the revocation or suspension of the operator’s permit at a hearing before OATH. This may result in the establishment being closed for a longer period of time, or permanently.
nlc: Verify  that the Health Department was prompted to initiate procedures for the revocation or suspension of an operator’s permit at a hearing before OATH due to repeated violations, which had result in the establishment being closed for a longer period of time, or permanently.

rule: Consumers may check an establishment’s inspection history.
nlc: Check if consumers can view an establishment's inspection history.

rule: As of July 2010, certain types of food service establishments, including restaurants, coffee shops, bars, nightclubs and most cafeterias and fixed-site food stands, must post letter grades that correspond to their sanitary inspection scores.
nlc: Verify that as of July 2010, certain types of food service establishments, including restaurants, coffee shops, bars, nightclubs, and most cafeterias and fixed-site food stands, are posting letter grades that correspond to their sanitary inspection scores.

rule: A score of 0-13 results in a grade of A; 14-27 points, a B; and 28 or more points, a C.
nlc: Verify that a score of 0-13 results in a grade of A, 14-27 points results in a B, and 28 or more points results in a C.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE (1.score >= 0 AND r1.score <= 13 AND r1.grade != 'A') OR (r1.score >= 14 AND r1.score <= 27 AND r1.grade != 'B') OR (r1.score >= 28 AND r1.grade != 'C')

rule: Letter grades are not issued to mobile food vending units, temporary food service establishments, food service establishments operated by primary or secondary schools, hospital-operated cafeterias, correctional facilities, charitable organizations (including soup kitchens or other prepared food distribution programs) or food service establishments operated by not-for-profit membership organizations that serve food only to their members.
nlc: Verify that letter grades are not issued to mobile food vending units, temporary food service establishments, food service establishments operated by primary or secondary schools, hospital-operated cafeterias, correctional facilities, charitable organizations (including soup kitchens or other prepared food distribution programs), or food service establishments operated by not-for-profit membership organizations that serve food only to their members.

rule: Restaurants with A grades are inspected less often than those with B or C grades
nlc: Check if restaurants with an A grade are inspected less often than those with B or C grades.

rule: Score 0 to 13 points = A; Score 14 to 27 points = B; Score 28 or more points = C
nlc: Verify that a score of 0 to 13 points is A, 14 to 27 points is B, and 28 or more points is C.

rule: Only certain inspections result in a grade.
nlc: Verify that only certain inspections result in a grade.

rule: Every food service establishment is scheduled for at least one inspection per year.
nlc: Verify that every food service establishment is scheduled for at least one inspection per year.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE r1.days_to_next_inspection > 365

rule: A restaurant that scores 0 to 13 violation points on its first inspection will receive an A-grade card that must be posted immediately.
nlc: Check if a restaurant that scored 0 to 13 violation points on its first inspection received an A-grade card that was posted immediately.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE r1.inspection_type LIKE '%Initial Inspection%' AND r1.score >= 0 AND r1.score <= 13 AND r1.grade = 'A' AND r1.grade_date != r1.inspection_date

rule: An establishment that does not score an A on its initial inspection will not have to post a grade until it has had the opportunity to improve its sanitary conditions and is re-inspected.
nlc: Verify that an establishment that does not score an A on its initial inspection is not required to post a grade until it has had the opportunity to improve its sanitary conditions and is re-inspected.

rule: If an A is issued on re-inspection, the A-grade card must be posted immediately.
nlc: Verify that if an A was issued on re-inspection, the A-grade card was posted immediately.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE r1.inspection_type LIKE '%Re-inspection%' AND r1.grade = 'A' AND r1.grade_date != r1.inspection_date

rule: An establishment receiving a B or C grade on re-inspection receives two cards: one showing the letter grade and one that says “Grade Pending”; one of those cards must be posted immediately.
nlc: Verify that an establishment receiving a B or C grade on re-inspection posts one of two cards immediately: either the card showing the letter grade (B or C) or the card that says “Grade Pending”.
sql: SELECT * FROM restaurant_inspection r1 WHERE r1.inspection_type LIKE '%Re-inspection%' AND r1.grade IN ('B', 'C') AND r1.grade_date != r1.inspection_date

rule: The frequency of inspections depends on a restaurant’s score.
nlc: Verify that the frequency of inspections depends on a restaurant's score.

rule: Restaurants with A grades are inspected less often than those with B or C grades.
nlc: Check if restaurants with A grades are inspected less frequently than those with B or C grades.
sql: SELECT r1.*, r2.* FROM restaurant_inspection r1, restaurant_inspection r2 WHERE r1.tid < r2.tid AND r1.camis = r2.camis AND r1.grade = 'A' AND r2.grade IN ('B', 'C') AND r1.days_to_next_inspection < r2.days_to_next_inspection

rule: The letter grade or “Grade Pending” card must be posted in a place where it is easily seen by people passing by. It must be on the front window, door or an outside-facing wall.
nlc: Verify that the letter grade or "Grade Pending" card is posted in a location that is easily seen by people passing by, specifically on the front window, door, or an outside-facing wall.

rule: The card must be within 5 feet from the front door or other entrance, and within 6 feet from the ground or floor.
nlc: Verify that the card is within 5 feet from the front door or other entrance and within 6 feet from the ground or floor.

rule: Follow the steps below to practice A-grade food safety and keep your customers safe from food-borne illness.
nlc: Verify that the steps below are followed to practice A-grade food safety and keep customers safe from food-borne illness.

rule: Be sure employees are trained in basic food safety and supervised by someone who has a food protection certificate.
nlc: Check if employees are trained in basic food safety and are supervised by someone who has a food protection certificate.

rule: Arrange work schedules so that a supervisor with a food protection certificate is on duty whenever your restaurant is receiving or preparing food, or is open to the public.
nlc: Verify that a supervisor with a food protection certificate is on duty whenever the restaurant is receiving or preparing food, or is open to the public.

rule: Train supervisors to use the Self-Inspection Worksheet to regularly evaluate and improve the restaurant's condition and employees' food safety practices.
nlc: Verify that supervisors are trained to use the Self-Inspection Worksheet to regularly evaluate and improve the restaurant's condition and employees' food safety practices.

rule: Provide food safety training for all employees who handle food.
nlc: Verify that all employees who handle food receive food safety training.

rule: Hold food at the proper temperature.
nlc: Verify that the food is being held at the proper temperature.

rule: Review Health Department rules for temperature-holding requirements.
nlc: Verify that Health Department rules for temperature-holding requirements are followed.

rule: Be sure equipment used to hold hot and cold food is working properly.
nlc: Verify that the equipment used to hold hot and cold food is working properly.

rule: Use thermometers to monitor the temperature of foods in hot or cold storage.
nlc: Verify that thermometers are being used to monitor the temperature of foods in hot or cold storage.

rule: Track food taken from hot or cold storage, and record how long it is out.
nlc: Verify that food taken from hot or cold storage is tracked and how long it is out is recorded.

rule: Control conditions that promote pests.
nlc: Verify that the conditions promoting pests are controlled.

rule: Seal all cracks, crevices and holes in walls, cabiners and doors to prevent rodents, cockroaches and flies from entering.
nlc: Verify that all cracks, crevices, and holes in walls, cabinets, and doors are sealed to prevent rodents, cockroaches, and flies from entering.

rule: Install rodent-proof door sweeps on outside doors.
nlc: Check if rodent-proof door sweeps have been installed on all outside doors.

rule: Store food and garbage in pest-proof containers.
nlc: Verify that food and garbage are stored in pest-proof containers.

rule: Clean grease, oil and food particles from all surfaces and equipment, including the floor underneath.
nlc: Verify that all surfaces and equipment, including the floor underneath, have been cleaned of grease, oil, and food particles.

rule: Keep range hoods clean and grease-free.
nlc: Verify that range hoods are kept clean and grease-free.

rule: Contract with a pest control professional licensed to work in restaurants.
nlc: Verify that the restaurant has a contract with a licensed pest control professional.

rule: Protect food from contamination during storage, preparation, transportation and display.
nlc: Verify that food is protected from contamination during storage, preparation, transportation, and display.

rule: Keep food covered until served.
nlc: Verify that food is kept covered until served.

rule: Keep food separated by temperature and type.
nlc: Verify that food is separated by temperature and type.

rule: Avoid cross-contamination by separating potentially hazardous foods (like raw poultry) from ready-to-eat items (like salad mix).
nlc: Verify that potentially hazardous foods (like raw poultry) are separated from ready-to-eat items (like salad mix) to avoid cross-contamination.

rule: Maintain all food surfaces.
nlc: Verify that all food surfaces are maintained.

rule: Clean and sanitize all food-preparation surfaces after each use; remove caked-on food.
nlc: Verify that all food-preparation surfaces are cleaned and sanitized after each use, and that caked-on food has been removed.

rule: Repair or replace deeply-grooved cutting boards and chipped or broken surfaces so they can be properly sanitized.
nlc: Verify that deeply-grooved cutting boards and chipped or broken surfaces are repaired or replaced so they can be properly sanitized.

rule: Maintain all non-food surfaces.
nlc: Verify that all non-food surfaces are maintained.

rule: Review Health Department rules on acceptable materials; surfaces should be smooth and cleanable.
nlc: Verify that all materials and surfaces are smooth and cleanable, as per Health Department rules.

rule: Keep all surfaces clean.
nlc: Verify that all surfaces are kept clean.

rule: Maintain all plumbing and check it frequently.
nlc: Verify that all plumbing is being maintained and checked frequently.

rule: Monitor all plumbing fixtures and make needed repairs immediately.
nlc: Verify that all plumbing fixtures are being monitored and that any needed repairs are being made immediately.

rule: Be sure plumbing is fitted with approved devices (valves, anti-siphonage pieces, vacuum breakers) to prevent backflow.
nlc: Verify that the plumbing is fitted with approved devices (valves, anti-siphonage pieces, vacuum breakers) to prevent backflow.

rule: Clean and maintain grease traps.
nlc: Verify that grease traps are cleaned and maintained.
